textByHashes.4270c2c8514a89cda403e4bab1eef8809553bc4f: Hello there. Interested in hearing the tale of Serafina?
textByHashes.aaa6b0b6f0490847713c42229ed556da07eb1d94: Great! Now, in case you didn't know, the castle before you is Ver Sinhaza. It's home to the Theatre of Blood.
textByHashes.901b6c013a85fd2f4478d43c283ee2926aba5962: The Theatre is run by a famous vampyre noble, Lady Verzik Vitur. She has promised that any citizen of Meiyerditch who survives the Theatre will be freed of the vampyre blood tithes.
textByHashes.9a1b9e85af25885b057855844519b572c5654379: The Theatre has been around for many years and thousands of people have tried their luck against it. However, in all that time, only one person has ever survived until the end.
textByHashes.6edec15c32966cb5449a81e3c3ecf7a9bdab99a9: Exactly. Serafina's younger brother was very ill and he wasn't going to survive many more blood tithes. She couldn't just let him die, so she did what no other person ever had.
textByHashes.60ed82d06e7b6fc5726fe61e16fac302ed3f3553: She entered the Theatre and succeeded in every challenge she faced. They say that Verzik wasn't too happy about that, but she kept her promise. Serafina and her brother were freed.
textByHashes.a435e87b71fb4ec0de123c443b2e17cebcaa9664: Well of course. When she survived the Theatre, she provided hope to every person in Meiyerditch. Hope that they can one day follow in her footsteps.
textByHashes.3c001aa6fef64faa13756c73f84d66d5d6101abf: Well I suppose she left Meiyerditch. Can't imagine any free person wanting to stick around here.
textByHashes.f5e3bce73055fd1b29ae82e2a3d17cbeed40b9cb: Well... No. It was a long time ago. Not many people around who remember it.
textByHashes.32c28c806dd4189c67924b70257eda1eb17225f2: It's called Ver Sinhaza. They say it was once the home of a Saradominist queen. Now it belongs to Verzik Vitur, a vampyre noble.
textByHashes.33dfc3702c5031514bd4291c17565de86c2c25c6: Beneath the castle you'll find the Theatre of Blood. Every day, citizens of Meiyerditch take on various challenges set up by Verzik, all the provide entertainment for the vampyres.
textByHashes.a340078389cc7a82523685ebe18605980fc4df14: The citizens don't need to be forced though. Anyone who survives the Theatre is promised freedom from the vampyres. It provides a little bit of hope in this dark place.
textByHashes.02c3bcbc28538aea0a902525536b9b3e53b6ddc3: I do. I'd rather not talk about them though. Those kinds of conversations get you in trouble.
textByHashes.f40fb22011e4cb80a47b7b950b6f4328eb7dff7a: Old Man Ral might be willing to talk to you about them though. He lives in sector one of Meiyerditch, directly west of here.
textByHashes.b5dc410205cddd223a01d312807cf58544ab68fb: Yep, that's where wee are at the moment. The region is split into two cities, Meiyerditch in the south and Darkmeyer in the north.
textByHashes.0e6cc4ff573d2ac6e1b57daf878b0f723d5f63c5: Meiyerditch is just one large ghetto. The city is split into sectors so that the vampyres can collect blood tithes on a rotational basis. That way they don't kill everyone off in one go.
textByHashes.7feaef098bfef633f8d967cce34663d705fc26e9: Darkmeyer is the vampyre capital. Humans aren't allowed there unless they're a personal slave of some lord or lady. They say the vampyres there live very luxurious lifestyles.
textByHashes.4eb454aeb1dca56a79ab878ce62482d0351a4dc5: Not much really. There seems to be three main types, juveniles, juvinates and vy*res.
textByHashes.e3ff6dd8e953add37793ac60cbb31d29aaf6ba99: Juveniles seem to be the lowest form of the vampyre. No one around here knows for sure but the general consensus is that they're former humans who've been converted in some way.
textByHashes.64d53f0f33233f4fd39fee4d744fc0c0fa44f878: Juvinates are initiated juveniles who've had some training and are starting to gain some powers.
textByHashes.35a19732a93221b55ca1d29b7a3609bec3c2bcdb: Then you have the vyres. Very nasty and very powerful. I wouldn't get on the wrong side of one if I were you.
textByHashes.13add930e6685be82c01bda2e25f8eb4f2ad0b23: Hmm, nothing has been seen of him for many years now. You can occasionally see Vanescula flying about, but not too often.
